task.objective: Remediate independent boundary-shape, kernel-owned reason-code semantic collision, and evaluation-scope consistency findings in KERNEL-VS-001 without widening Phase 1 scope or changing authoritative Proofrail product semantics.

requirements: Materialize governance/tasks/KERNEL-VS-CONV-001.json as the first task artifact.
Preserve KERNEL-VS-001 product semantics.
Do not modify canonical terminology, Verdict semantics, Evidence authority classes, Trust semantics, protocol authority, or Phase 1 authorization.
Resolve KVS-BND-001.
Every authoritative Array container must be structurally validated before any array element value is read.
The scanner MUST NOT call forEach, map, for-of, spread, Array.from, or another element-reading iteration mechanism on an authoritative Array before inspecting the Array container descriptors.
Add a bounded Array-container structural validation helper.
The helper must inspect own property descriptors and own keys before reading element values.
A valid authoritative Array container may contain the ordinary own length property required by Array and own data properties for every canonical integer index from 0 through length - 1.
Reject sparse authoritative Arrays.
Every index from 0 through length - 1 must exist as an own data property.
Reject accessor-backed Array elements before invoking the getter or setter.
Reject symbol-keyed Array properties.
Reject unexpected extra string-keyed Array properties.
Reject non-enumerable custom Array properties. The built-in Array length property is the expected special non-enumerable property and must not itself be rejected.

Apply the Array-container validation recursively to all authoritative arrays, including at minimum claims, evidenceContracts, evidenceRequirements, observations, rules, Evidence Contract requirementIds, and Observation limitations.
A forbidden authority-shaped property attached to an Array container, including modelConfidence, inferenceProposal, and proposedContent, must produce FORBIDDEN_AUTHORITY_FIELD before evaluation.
Array shape failures must produce KernelBoundaryError. They must not escape later as ordinary TypeError from normalization, Evidence satisfaction, Rule evaluation, or bundle finalization.

Add focused tests for modelConfidence attached as an enumerable property to observations Array; inferenceProposal attached to rules Array; symbol-keyed property attached to an authoritative Array; non-enumerable custom property attached to an authoritative Array; accessor-backed numeric Array index; sparse observations Array; sparse rules Array; unexpected ordinary string-keyed Array property; nested sparse Evidence Contract requirementIds Array; nested accessor-backed Observation limitations Array.
The accessor-backed numeric-index test must prove the getter body was not executed. Use an external counter or equivalent side-effect sentinel and assert it remains unchanged.
Repeated validation of the same malformed Array shape must produce the same KernelBoundaryError issue category and relative input path.
Preserve acceptance of ordinary dense JSON-compatible Arrays.
Resolve KVS-RSN-001.
KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING is a kernel-owned condition reason code for the missing Evidence Requirement classification.
A synthetic Rule MUST NOT declare KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING as its Rule effect reasonCode.
Reserve the exact code KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING from Rule-supplied reason codes.
Do not create a general product reason-code registry.
Do not reserve every KERNEL_ reason-code prefix. Existing narrow synthetic Rule reason codes using KERNEL_ may remain valid.
Prefer a small internal kernel reason-code module or equivalent single source of truth so Evidence satisfaction and boundary validation do not duplicate the reserved literal independently.
The kernel-owned condition reason-code declaration remains internal to packages/kernel. Do not move product evaluation behavior into packages/contracts.
Rule use of the reserved missing-Evidence code must fail at the public kernel boundary with KernelBoundaryError.
Prefer issue category RESERVED_KERNEL_REASON_CODE unless an existing KernelBoundaryError category has exactly correct semantics.

Add focused tests proving a Rule using KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING is rejected before Rule evaluation; the missing Evidence Requirement classifier still emits KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING; a normal Rule reason code such as KERNEL_SYNTHETIC_DENIAL remains valid; and a Rule denial cannot produce REJECTED with KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING as its Rule-supplied reason.
Preserve HARN_ namespace rejection.
Resolve KVS-SCOPE-001.
Observation target scope must belong to the declared evaluation scope.

For this Phase 1 vertical slice, the declared evaluation scope is the set of targetScopeId values covered by Claims and selected Evidence Contracts after existing Claim/Contract consistency validation.
An Observation whose targetScopeId does not belong to that declared evaluation scope must be rejected before normalization.
Prefer the existing TARGET_SCOPE_MISMATCH KernelBoundaryError category.

Preserve the distinction between an Observation in the correct evaluation scope that does not satisfy a Requirement because observer, observer version, fact key, fact value, or limitations differ and an Observation whose target scope is outside the declared evaluation scope.
The first case remains an accepted evaluation input that may remain unaccepted for Evidence satisfaction.
The second case is malformed boundary input and must be rejected.

Add focused tests proving unrelated Observation target scope is rejected; wrong observer in a valid target scope remains valid boundary input and produces missing-Evidence behavior; wrong fact key in a valid target scope remains valid boundary input and produces missing-Evidence behavior; and a limited Observation in a valid target scope remains valid boundary input and does not satisfy the Requirement.
Preserve all previously implemented deterministic behavior.

Do not weaken Claim is not Evidence, exact Observation satisfaction matching, primitive type distinction, limitation handling, Evidence Contract selection provenance restrictions, Rule authority provenance restrictions, duplicate identity rejection, invalid reference rejection, Claim/Contract scope coverage, Contract/Requirement bidirectional membership, Verdict precedence, lower-precedence reason retention, deterministic normalization, canonical serialization, deterministic SHA-256-derived identities, Evidence Lineage coverage, caller-input isolation, deep bundle immutability, forbidden model/inference authority fields, HARN_ namespace separation, public kernel surface restrictions, or package dependency direction.

requiredArtifacts: Array descriptor validation before element reads
focused negative and regression kernel tests

requiredInvariants: No authoritative Array element accessor can execute before boundary rejection.
Sparse authoritative Arrays cannot reach normalization or evaluation.
Array-attached forbidden authority fields cannot be silently discarded.
Every malformed Array-container regression produces KernelBoundaryError.
KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING remains the missing Evidence Requirement condition reason code.
HARN_ Rule reason codes remain rejected.
An Observation outside the declared evaluation target scope is rejected.
An Observation inside the declared scope may remain unmatched without being rejected solely because it does not satisfy an Evidence Requirement.
Identical normalized valid inputs continue producing deeply equal bundle output and stable bundle identity.
No production package outside contracts and kernel exists.
No repository inspection, target-code execution, verification execution, Policy runtime, adapter, delivery, network, or inference path is introduced.

stopConditions: a general product reason-code registry is required
a new production package is required
a new external dependency is required
PR #5 or exact reviewed head identity differs from preflight
